BG 16.B#ॐ श्री परमात्मने नमः\nअथ षोडशोऽध्यायः दैवासुर सम्पद् विभाग योगः#om sri paramatmane namaha\natha shodashodhyayaha daivasura sampad vibhaga yogaha#Salutations to the most auspicious divine in all. Henceforth, the sixteenth chapter named Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga#om sri paramatmane—the most auspicious divine in all
column5: daivasura sampad vibhaga yogaha—Yoga of the divine and demoniac natures

column1:
BG 16.1#श्रीभगवानुवाच |
अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थिति: |
दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ||1||
अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्याग: शान्तिरपैशुनम् |
दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ||2||
तेज: क्षमा धृति: शौचमद्रोहोनातिमानिता |
भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ||3||#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
abhayaṁ sattva-sanśhuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥ
dānaṁ damaśh cha yajñaśh cha svādhyāyas tapa ārjavam
ahinsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śhāntir apaiśhunam
dayā bhūteṣhv aloluptvaṁ mārdavaṁ hrīr achāpalam
tejaḥ kṣhamā dhṛitiḥ śhaucham adroho nāti-mānitā
bhavanti sampadaṁ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata#BG 16.1-3:#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Divine Personality said

BG 16.2#श्रीभगवानुवाच |
अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थिति: |
दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ||1||
अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्याग: शान्तिरपैशुनम् |
दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ||2||
तेज: क्षमा धृति: शौचमद्रोहोनातिमानिता |
भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ||3||#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha

BG 16.3#श्रीभगवानुवाच |
अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थिति: |
दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ||1||
अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्याग: शान्तिरपैशुनम् |
दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ||2||
तेज: क्षमा धृति: शौचमद्रोहोनातिमानिता |
भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ||3||#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha

bhavanti sampadaṁ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata#BG 16.1-3:#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Divine Personality said

BG 16.4#दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोध: पारुष्यमेव च |\nअज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ||4||#dambho darpo ’bhimānaśh cha krodhaḥ pāruṣhyam eva cha\najñānaṁ chābhijātasya pārtha sampadam āsurīm#O Parth, the qualities of those who possess a demoniac nature are hypocrisy, arrogance, conceit, anger, harshness, and ignorance.#dambhaḥ—hypocrisy

BG 16.5#दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता |\nमा शुच: सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ||5||#daivī sampad vimokṣhāya nibandhāyāsurī matā\nmā śhuchaḥ sampadaṁ daivīm abhijāto ’si pāṇḍava#The divine qualities lead to liberation, while the demoniac qualities are the cause for a continuing destiny of bondage. Grieve not, O Arjun, as you were born with saintly virtues.#daivī—divine

BG 16.6#द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन्दैव आसुर एव च |\nदैवो विस्तरश: प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे शृणु ||6||#dvau bhūta-sargau loke ’smin daiva āsura eva cha\ndaivo vistaraśhaḥ prokta āsuraṁ pārtha me śhṛiṇu#There are two kinds of beings in this world—those endowed with a divine nature and those possessing a demoniac nature. I have described the divine qualities in detail, O Arjun. Now hear from me about the demoniac nature.#dvau—two

BG 16.7#प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुरा: |\nन शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते ||7||#pravṛittiṁ cha nivṛittiṁ cha janā na vidur āsurāḥ\nna śhauchaṁ nāpi chāchāro na satyaṁ teṣhu vidyate#Those possessing a demoniac nature do not comprehend which actions are proper and which are improper. Hence, they possess neither purity, nor good conduct, nor even truthfulness.#pravṛittim—proper actions

BG 16.8#असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम् |\nअपरस्परसम्भूतं किमन्यत्कामहैतुकम् ||8||#asatyam apratiṣhṭhaṁ te jagad āhur anīśhvaram\naparaspara-sambhūtaṁ kim anyat kāma-haitukam#They say, “The world is without Absolute Truth, without any basis (for moral order), and without a God (who has created or is controlling it). It is created from the combination of the two sexes, and has no purpose other than sexual gratification.”#asatyam—without Absolute Truth

BG 16.9#एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धय: |\nप्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्माण: क्षयाय जगतोऽहिता: ||9||#etāṁ dṛiṣhṭim avaṣhṭabhya naṣhṭātmāno ’lpa-buddhayaḥ\nprabhavanty ugra-karmāṇaḥ kṣhayāya jagato ’hitāḥ#Holding fast to such views, these misdirected souls, with small intellect and cruel actions, arise as enemies of the world threatening its destruction.#etām—such

BG 16.10#काममाश्रित्य दुष्पूरं दम्भमानमदान्विता: |\nमोहाद्गृहीत्वासद्ग्राहान्प्रवर्तन्तेऽशुचिव्रता: ||10||#kāmam āśhritya duṣhpūraṁ dambha-māna-madānvitāḥ\nmohād gṛihītvāsad-grāhān pravartante ’śhuchi-vratāḥ#Harboring insatiable lust, full of hypocrisy, pride and arrogance, the demoniac cling to their false tenets. Thus illusioned, they are attracted to the impermanent and work with impure resolve.#kāmam—lust

BG 16.11#चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिता: |\nकामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्चिता: ||11||#chintām aparimeyāṁ cha pralayāntām upāśhritāḥ\nkāmopabhoga-paramā etāvad iti niśhchitāḥ#They are obsessed with endless anxieties that end only with death. Still, they maintain with complete assurance that gratification of desires and accumulation of wealth is the highest purpose of life.#chintām—anxieties

BG 16.12#आशापाशशतैर्बद्धा: कामक्रोधपरायणा: |\nईहन्ते कामभोगार्थमन्यायेनार्थसञ्जयान् ||12||#āśhā-pāśha-śhatair baddhāḥ kāma-krodha-parāyaṇāḥ\nīhante kāma-bhogārtham anyāyenārtha-sañchayān#Held in bondage by hundreds of desires, and driven by lust and anger, they strive to accumulate wealth by unjust means, all for the gratification of their senses.#āśhā-pāśha—bondage of desires

BG 16.13#इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् |
इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् ||13||
असौ मया हत: शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि |
ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी ||14||
आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया |
यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिता: ||15||#idam adya mayā labdham imaṁ prāpsye manoratham
idam astīdam api me bhaviṣhyati punar dhanam
asau mayā hataḥ śhatrur haniṣhye chāparān api
īśhvaro ’ham ahaṁ bhogī siddho ’haṁ balavān sukhī
āḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛiśho mayā
yakṣhye dāsyāmi modiṣhya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ#BG 16.13-15:#idam—this

BG 16.14#इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् |
इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् ||13||
असौ मया हत: शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि |
ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी ||14||
आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया |
यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिता: ||15||#idam adya mayā labdham imaṁ prāpsye manoratham
idam astīdam api me bhaviṣhyati punar dhanam
asau mayā hataḥ śhatrur haniṣhye chāparān api
īśhvaro ’ham ahaṁ bhogī siddho ’haṁ balavān sukhī
āḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛiśho mayā
yakṣhye dāsyāmi modiṣhya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ#BG 16.13-15:#idam—this

BG 16.15#इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् |
इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् ||13||
असौ मया हत: शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि |
ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी ||14||
आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया |
यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिता: ||15||#idam adya mayā labdham imaṁ prāpsye manoratham
idam astīdam api me bhaviṣhyati punar dhanam
asau mayā hataḥ śhatrur haniṣhye chāparān api
īśhvaro ’ham ahaṁ bhogī siddho ’haṁ balavān sukhī
āḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛiśho mayā
yakṣhye dāsyāmi modiṣhya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ#BG 16.13-15:#idam—this

BG 16.16#अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृता: |\nप्रसक्ता: कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ ||16||#aneka-chitta-vibhrāntā moha-jāla-samāvṛitāḥ\nprasaktāḥ kāma-bhogeṣhu patanti narake ’śhuchau#Possessed and led astray by such imaginings, enveloped in a mesh of delusion, and addicted to the gratification of sensuous pleasures, they descend to the murkiest hell.#aneka—many

BG 16.17#आत्मसम्भाविता: स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विता: |\nयजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्वकम् ||17||#ātma-sambhāvitāḥ stabdhā dhana-māna-madānvitāḥ\nyajante nāma-yajñais te dambhenāvidhi-pūrvakam#Such self-conceited and stubborn people, full of pride and arrogant in their wealth, perform ostentatious sacrifices in name only, with no regard to the rules of the scriptures.#ātma-sambhāvitāḥ—self-conceited
column12: avidhi-pūrvakam—with no regards to the rules of the scriptures

column1: BG 16.18#अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिता: |\nमामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयका: ||18||#ahankāraṁ balaṁ darpaṁ kāmaṁ krodhaṁ cha sanśhritāḥ\nmām ātma-para-deheṣhu pradviṣhanto ’bhyasūyakāḥ#Blinded by egotism, strength, arrogance, desire, and anger, the demonic abuse Me, who am present in their own body and in the bodies of others.#ahankāram—egotism
column9: ātma-para-deheṣhu—within one’s own and bodies of others

column1:
BG 16.19#तानहं द्विषत: क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् |
क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु ||19||
आसुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि |
मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ||20||#tān ahaṁ dviṣhataḥ krūrān sansāreṣhu narādhamān
kṣhipāmy ajasram aśhubhān āsurīṣhv eva yoniṣhu
āsurīṁ yonim āpannā mūḍhā janmani janmani
mām aprāpyaiva kaunteya tato yānty adhamāṁ gatim#BG 16.19-20:#tān—these
column6: nara-adhamān—the vile and vicious of humankind

column1:
BG 16.20#तानहं द्विषत: क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् |
क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु ||19||
आसुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि |
मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ||20||#tān ahaṁ dviṣhataḥ krūrān sansāreṣhu narādhamān
kṣhipāmy ajasram aśhubhān āsurīṣhv eva yoniṣhu
āsurīṁ yonim āpannā mūḍhā janmani janmani
mām aprāpyaiva kaunteya tato yānty adhamāṁ gatim#BG 16.19-20:#tān—these
column6: nara-adhamān—the vile and vicious of humankind

column1: BG 16.21#त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मन: |\nकाम: क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् ||21||#tri-vidhaṁ narakasyedaṁ dvāraṁ nāśhanam ātmanaḥ\nkāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṁ tyajet#There are three gates leading to the hell of self-destruction for the soul—lust, anger, and greed. Therefore, one should abandon all three.#tri-vidham—three types of

BG 16.22#एतैर्विमुक्त: कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नर: |\nआचरत्यात्मन: श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ||22||#etair vimuktaḥ kaunteya tamo-dvārais tribhir naraḥ\nācharaty ātmanaḥ śhreyas tato yāti parāṁ gatim#Those who are free from the three gates to darkness endeavor for the welfare of their soul, and thereby attain the supreme goal.#etaiḥ—from this

BG 16.23#य: शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारत: |\nन स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् ||23||#yaḥ śhāstra-vidhim utsṛijya vartate kāma-kārataḥ\nna sa siddhim avāpnoti na sukhaṁ na parāṁ gatim#Those who act under the impulse of desire, discarding the injunctions of the scriptures, attain neither perfection, nor happiness, nor the supreme goal in life.#yaḥ—who

BG 16.24#तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ |\nज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि ||24||#tasmāch chhāstraṁ pramāṇaṁ te kāryākārya-vyavasthitau\njñātvā śhāstra-vidhānoktaṁ karma kartum ihārhasi#Therefore, let the scriptures be your authority in determining what should be done and what should not be done. Understand the scriptural injunctions and teachings, and then perform your actions in this world accordingly.#tasmāt—therefore

BG 16.E#ॐ तत् सत् इति श्रीमत् भगवत् गीतासु उपनिषत्सु
ब्रह्म-विद्यायां योग-शास्त्रे श्रीकृष्ण-अर्जुन-संवादे
दैवासुर-सम्पद्-विभाग योगो नाम षोडशोऽध्यायः#Om tat sat iti
Srimad Bhagavad Gitasu Upanishatsu
Brahma Vidyayam yogashastre Sri-Krishna-arujuna samvade
Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yogo Nama shodashodhyaha#Om Truth Is. Thus in the Upanishads of the glorious Bhagavad Gita, the science of the Eternal, the scripture of Yoga, the dialogue between Sri Krishna and Arjuna ends the Sixteenth discourse entitled The Yoga of the Divine and Demoniac Natures.#Om—Om
column11: Sri Krishna arujuna samvade—a dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna
column12: Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yogo Nama—Yoga of the Divine and Demoniac Natures
